sections.paragraph:
δίκη, ης, ἡ (Hom.+; ins, pap, LXX; TestAbr A 2 p. 78, 18 [Stone p. 4] δίκην ‘like’; TestSol 1:9 and ApcSed 3:1 ‘complaint’ or ‘charge’; Philo, Joseph., Just., Ath. [the last also δίκην ‘like’]; loanw. in rabb. Originally=‘direction, way’, hence adverbial acc. δίκην ‘in manner of, like’ w. gen.; Hom.=what is right, esp. in terms of custom or usage.)

① punishment meted out as legal penalty, punishment, penalty (Pre-Socr., Trag.+; pap, e.g. UPZ 1, 13 [IV b.c.]; PFay 21, 24 [II a.d.]; LXX) δίκην τίνειν (Trag.; Pla.; Epict. 3, 24, 4; 3, 26, 20; Plut., Mor. 553f; 559d; 561b; 592e; Aelian, VH 9, 8; Philo, Spec. Leg. 3, 175, Mos. 1, 245; Just., A I, 17, 4; Ath., R. 74, 2) pay a penalty, suffer punishment, be punished τὶ of or with someth. 2 Th 1:9; Hm 2:5; Hs 9, 19, 3. Also δ. διδόναι (freq. Trag.+; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 143, Vi. 343; Just., D. 1:5) D 1:5 (δ. διδόναι of divine punishment also Diod S 16, 31, 4. But s. RKraft, The Didache and Barnabas ’65, p. 141 will be called to account) and δ. ὑπέχειν (not infreq. since Soph., Oed. Rex 552; also Diog. L. 3, 79; Jos., Ant. 14, 45; 168) Jd 7; δ. αἰτεῖσθαι κατά τινος Ac 25:15 v.l.

② Justice personified as a deity Ac 28:4 (Soph., Ant. 538 ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἐάσαι τοῦτο γʼ ἡ Δίκη σʼ, ἐπεὶ κτλ.; Ael. Aristid. 52 p. 606 D.; Arrian, Anab. 4, 9, 7 Δίκη as πάρεδρος of Zeus; Pind., O. 7, 17 al.; Pla., Leg. 716a; Plotin. V 8, 4, 40ff; Damasc., Vi. Isid. 138; Procop. Soph., Ep. 17; 149; Herm. Wr. p. 420, 1 Sc.; 4 Macc; Philo; Joseph.; Warnecke, Romfahrt 118f).—RHirzel, Themis, Dike u. Verwandtes 1907; WJaeger, Paideia ’34, 144ff (s. Eng. tr. ’39, vol. 1, 68); DLoenen, Dike ’48; Kl. Pauly II 24–26.—B. 1358. DELG. LfgrE s.v. (lit.). DDD 476–80. M-M. TW. Spicq. Sv. S. δικάζω.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

acc. acc. = accusative

gen. gen. = genitive

esp. esp. = especially

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Hm Hm = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Warnecke, Romfahrt Warnecke, Romfahrt = HW., Die tatsächliche Romfahrt des Apostels Paulus—List 6

Eng. tr. Eng. tr. = English translation

vol. vol. = volume

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

LfgrE LfgrE = Lexicon des frühgriechischen Epos—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 250
